channel.title: Posts on Ben Santora
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Ben Santora

item.title: How to Run llama.cpp Bare-Metal on Linux for Maximum CPU Inference Performance
item.description: Compiling llama.cpp from source with native CPU optimizations eliminates wrapper overhead and maximizes inference performance on Linux systems without dedicated GPUs. This guide walks through every step — from auditing your hardware to running your first local conversation. What Is the Difference Between llama.cpp and Ollama? llama.cpp is the inference engine: a raw C++ implementation of the Llama architecture that performs the core matrix math of AI inference directly on your hardware.
